input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to benchmark three methods (AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, UniqueWordCount) from TextAnalyzer.cs, comparing .NET 9 vs .NET 10 performance. Let me start by exploring the codebase and checking what .NET versions are available.

data.content: Good — both .NET 9 and .NET 10 SDKs/runtimes are available. Let me read the source files.
Now I'll create a BenchmarkDotNet project that multi-targets net9.0 and net10.0.
data.reasoningText: Now I need to create a BenchmarkDotNet project that benchmarks the three methods (AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, UniqueWordCount) on both .NET 9 and .NET 10. I'll create a benchmark project that multi-targets net9.0 and net10.0.

data.content: The BDN version doesn't have the .NET 10 moniker yet. Let me fix that with a custom job config.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 might not have `Net100` yet. Let me check what the correct moniker is or use a different approach. I could use custom jobs with explicit framework specification, or use the `[SimpleJob]` with a different approach.

Let me try using environment variables or a different approach. Actually, BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 uses `Net100` starting from a specific version. Let me check if there's a newer version, or I'll use a custom job configuration.

Actually, I think the moniker for .NET 10 might be `Net10_0` in newer BDN versions. 

I'll switch to using custom jobs instead to have more control over the framework targeting.

data.content: BDN 0.14.0 doesn't recognize .NET 10. Let me try a newer version that might support it.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 doesn't support .NET 10 yet. I have a few options:

1. Use a newer BenchmarkDotNet version that supports .NET 10
2. Write a manual benchmark harness instead

Let me try a newer BDN version first. Let me check what's available on NuGet. Actually, BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 was released some time ago. Let me check if there's a newer version available.

Actually, let me just write a simple manual benchmark instead. It'll be faster and more reliable than trying to get BDN to work with .NET 10. I'll create two separate projects (one for net9.0, one for net10.0) or a single multi-target project with a manual stopwatch-based benchmark.


Since the system might be under load, I should build something more robust with proper warmup cycles and multiple iterations to get reliable measurements.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet package search BenchmarkDotNet --take 1 --exact-match 2>&1 | head -20", "description": "Check latest BenchmarkDotNet version"}

toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet package search BenchmarkDotNet --take 1 --exact-match 2>&1 | tail -10", "description": "Check latest BDN version (tail)"}

data.content: 0.15.8 is latest. Let me upgrade to that — it should support .NET 10.
